forecast.name: This Afternoon
forecast.detailedForecast: Rain showers likely after 2pm. Mostly cloudy, with a high near 80. South wind around 7 mph. Chance of precipitation is 50%. New rainfall amounts less than a tenth of an inch possible.

forecast.name: Tonight
forecast.detailedForecast: Rain showers likely before 8pm, then showers and thunderstorms between 8pm and 9pm, then patchy fog and showers and thunderstorms between 9pm and 11pm, then patchy fog and showers and thunderstorms. Cloudy, with a low around 61. South wind around 9 mph. Chance of precipitation is 90%. New rainfall amounts between a quarter and half of an inch possible.

weather_report:
In Boston today, the air hangs heavy with the promise of rain, a prelude to the thunderous symphony expected this evening. The clouds, thick and brooding, usher in a humid warmth, reaching towards that balmy 80 degrees as the afternoon unfolds. There’s a tangible weight to the atmosphere, a sense of anticipation swirling with the breezy whisper of south winds. It is a time when even the most intrepid may find themselves reaching for an umbrella, as rain showers are expected to creep in after 2 PM, teasing with a 50% chance of gentle drizzle.

As dusk approaches, the rhythm of the day will shift; expect a crescendo of rainfall and the rumble of distant thunder, an announcement from nature itself. The temperature will settle into a comfortable embrace as evening falls, dipping to a cool 61. The air will turn thick with fog, lurking softly alongside those intermittent storms, creating an intriguing dance of mist and precipitation. Dress for the unpredictability: a breathable raincoat paired with some well-worn shoes should suffice to navigate the city’s avenues, while a cozy layer beneath offers warmth against the encroaching night. This ambiance invites an evening spent indoors, perhaps immersed in a good book or savoring a warm drink, allowing the pitter-patter of rain to be the backdrop to your reflections.
